text: Fill in the blanks about chromosomal aberrations and clinical genetics
questions: Down syndrome is caused by *trisomy/trisomy 21* of chromosome 21, and its incidence increases with advanced *maternal/mother* age.
Turner syndrome has a karyotype of *45X/45,X* and presents with short stature, webbed neck, and *ovarian/gonadal* dysgenesis in phenotypic females.
A *Robertsonian/robertsonian* translocation involves fusion of two acrocentric chromosomes at their centromeres, and balanced carriers have *45/forty-five* chromosomes.
Mosaicism occurs due to *nondisjunction/non-disjunction* during early mitotic divisions after fertilisation, producing two or more genetically *different/distinct* cell lines in one individual.
